# Generated Workflow Contract

<!-- Generated by scripts/generate-routing.py from agent-catalog.json. Do not edit by hand. -->

Primary entrypoint: `$protocol`  
Delegation entrypoint: `$launcher`

| Request type | Specialist sequence |
| --- | --- |
| Defect | `qa -> me -> qa` |
| Technical feature | `ta -> me -> qa` |
| Experience feature | `sd -> uxd -> uids -> uid -> ta -> me -> qa` |
| Physical-digital feature | `ind -> sd -> uxd -> uids -> uid -> ta -> me -> qa` |
| UI polish | `uids -> uid -> qa` |
| Security-sensitive feature | `ta -> sec -> me -> qa` |
| Infrastructure | `do -> me -> qa` |

## Routing edges

- `any -> ta` when Architecture decisions, decomposition, migration, or non-trivial technical planning is needed.
- `any -> me` when Implementation is ready after framing.
- `any -> qa` when Verification, tests, regression analysis, or design-fidelity evidence is needed.
- `any -> doc` when Durable documentation is needed.
- `any -> sec` when Auth, permissions, secrets, unsafe input, PII, or trust boundaries are in scope.
- `ind -> sd` when Physical-digital product direction needs service journey framing.
- `sd -> uxd` when Service concept is ready for task flow and interaction design.
- `uxd -> uids` when Task flow, states, and product language need visual hierarchy.
- `uids -> uid` when Visual system and state styling are clear enough to implement.
- `uid -> ta` when UI implementation boundaries need technical decomposition or integration planning.
- `ta -> me` when Implementation boundaries and test expectations are ready.
- `me -> qa` when Implementation work is complete and needs verification.
